root_text.an4.223:1.1: “Catūhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bālo abyatto asappuriso khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, sāvajjo ca hoti sānuvajjo viññūnaṁ, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavati.

translation_text.an4.223:1.1: “When a foolish, incompetent untrue person has four qualities they keep themselves broken and damaged. They deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they brim with much wickedness.
translation_text.an4.223:1.3: Bad conduct by way of body, speech, and mind, and being ungrateful and thankless.
translation_text.an4.223:1.4: An astute person … brims with much merit. … Good conduct by way of body, speech, and mind, and being grateful and thankful. …”
